items.reachability_status: Readable via powercfg. Current AC=0; DC=2
items.what_can_go_wrong: Can change heat, battery life, fan behavior, stability, latency, or benchmark results depending on the setting.
items.how_to_back_it_up: Export or clone the active power scheme before writing.
items.how_to_restore_it: Re-import or reactivate the backed-up power scheme, or set the previous AC/DC values.
items.suggested_warning_label: Changes CPU boost behavior. Can affect heat, fan noise, battery life, and stability.
items.suggested_default_enabled_state: Disabled for writes; visible as read-only until Phase 2+ confirmation.

items.reachability_status: Likely reachable if MSIAfterburner.exe is present
items.what_can_go_wrong: Wrong slot may apply an unintended GPU voltage/frequency curve or power behavior.
items.how_to_back_it_up: Back up MSIAfterburner.cfg and Profiles folder before launch testing.
items.how_to_restore_it: Launch known stock/safe MSI profile or restore backed-up MSI files.
items.suggested_warning_label: Applies an external MSI Afterburner profile slot. Verify slot mapping first.
items.suggested_default_enabled_state: Disabled until manually verified.

items.reachability_status: Requires MSI Afterburner or future manual profile-file research
items.what_can_go_wrong: Unstable clocks, driver resets, crashes, poor performance, or excessive heat if misconfigured.
items.how_to_back_it_up: Back up MSI profile files and record current NVIDIA telemetry before changes.
items.how_to_restore_it: Apply stock MSI profile and restore backed-up profile files.
items.suggested_warning_label: Advanced GPU curve control. Changes can crash games or reset the driver.
items.suggested_default_enabled_state: Disabled; require explicit confirmation.

items.reachability_status: Reachable when nvidia-smi works
items.what_can_go_wrong: Overly aggressive polling can add overhead or noisy logs.
items.how_to_back_it_up: No system backup needed; preserve log configuration.
items.how_to_restore_it: Stop polling and delete generated logs if desired.
items.suggested_warning_label: Read-only GPU telemetry. Use sane polling intervals.
items.suggested_default_enabled_state: Enabled for read-only dashboard if available.

items.reachability_status: Optional tool; discovered or missing per report
items.current_phase_status: Not used for capture in Phase 1
items.what_can_go_wrong: Wrong process targeting can capture no data or the wrong app; high-frequency logs can grow large.
items.how_to_back_it_up: Keep capture outputs in timestamped session folders.
items.how_to_restore_it: Stop capture process and archive/delete session logs.
items.suggested_warning_label: Captures frame-time logs for the selected process.
items.suggested_default_enabled_state: Disabled until user starts a benchmark session.

items.reachability_status: Known feasible from previous project helpers; not expanded in this app Phase 1
items.what_can_go_wrong: Large logs or misleading results if endpoint choice is poor.
items.how_to_back_it_up: No system backup required; keep log files with session metadata.
items.how_to_restore_it: Stop logger and archive/delete logs.
items.suggested_warning_label: Read-only network logging. Does not change adapter settings.
items.suggested_default_enabled_state: Optional per session.

items.setting_control_name: Fan mode/control through GCC or OEM paths
items.reachability_status: Unknown; direct control not proven
items.what_can_go_wrong: Unexpected fan behavior, high temperatures, OEM service conflicts, or persistent config changes.
items.how_to_back_it_up: Export app/OEM config if proven safe; record current mode manually first.
items.how_to_restore_it: Use OEM app to return to default/auto fan mode; reboot if needed.
items.suggested_warning_label: Fan control is experimental until an official reversible path is proven.
items.suggested_default_enabled_state: Read-only/disabled.

items.reachability_status: Not attempted; research only
items.what_can_go_wrong: Can destabilize hardware control, break fan behavior, or require reboot/recovery.
items.how_to_back_it_up: No reliable generic backup; requires dedicated research and OEM documentation.
items.how_to_restore_it: Reboot, OEM defaults, or vendor service recovery may be required.
items.suggested_warning_label: Dangerous low-level hardware access. Research only.
items.suggested_default_enabled_state: Hidden from apply path but visible in Experimental read-only notes.

items.reachability_status: Feasible later through scheduled tasks or startup entries
items.what_can_go_wrong: Bad automation can apply presets at the wrong time or make login noisy.
items.how_to_back_it_up: Export scheduled task/startup entry definitions before modification.
items.how_to_restore_it: Disable/remove created task or startup entry.
items.suggested_warning_label: Automation can change system behavior on login or game launch.
items.suggested_default_enabled_state: Disabled until explicitly configured.

items.setting_control_name: Game detection and auto preset switching
items.reachability_status: Readable through process enumeration; automation planned
items.what_can_go_wrong: False positives can apply the wrong preset if future writes are enabled.
items.how_to_back_it_up: Save automation rules as JSON before edits.
items.how_to_restore_it: Disable the game rule or restore previous JSON rules.
items.suggested_warning_label: Auto switching should require clear game/process matches.
items.suggested_default_enabled_state: Detection enabled; auto-apply disabled.

items.setting_control_name: Save current state / restore previous state
items.reachability_status: Planned; feasible for files and powercfg exports
items.what_can_go_wrong: Incomplete backups can create false confidence.
items.how_to_back_it_up: Save power plan export, MSI files, preset JSON, app settings, and command logs.
items.how_to_restore_it: Run restore workflow and verify telemetry/settings after restore.
items.suggested_warning_label: Always save state before applying tuning.
items.suggested_default_enabled_state: Enabled by default before any apply action.
